lessons.title: Describing Materials
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Describing materials by their observable properties: color, shape, texture, and size.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Everything around you is made of materials. A wooden table, a metal spoon, a cotton shirt, and a glass cup are all made from different materials. In this lesson, you will learn how to describe materials by their properties.

## Main Content

**Properties of Materials**

Materials have properties that help us describe them. Color tells us what a material looks like. Shape tells us its form. Texture tells us how it feels when we touch it. Size tells us how big or small it is. By observing these properties, we can identify and classify different materials.

blocks.content: Everything around you is made of materials. A wooden table, a metal spoon, a cotton shirt, and a glass cup are all made from different materials. In this lesson, you will learn how to describe materials by their properties.

terms.definition: A substance that things are made from
A special quality or feature of a material

terms.thai: เนื้อสัมผัส
terms.definition: How a material feels when you touch it
A material that is difficult to press or change shape
A material that is easy to press or change shape

lessons.titleThai: การบรรยายวัสดุ

lessons.title: Classifying Materials
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Classifying materials into groups based on their properties.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Scientists classify materials into groups based on their properties. This helps us organize and understand the world around us. In this lesson, you will learn how to sort materials into groups.

## Main Content

**Sorting Materials**

We can classify materials by their state: solid, liquid, or gas. We can also classify them by their properties: hard or soft, transparent or opaque, rough or smooth. Grouping materials by shared properties helps scientists study and use them more effectively.

blocks.content: Scientists classify materials into groups based on their properties. This helps us organize and understand the world around us. In this lesson, you will learn how to sort materials into groups.

terms.definition: To sort things into groups based on shared properties
A material that lets light pass through it
A material that does not let light pass through
A material with an uneven surface

lessons.titleThai: การจำแนกวัสดุ

lessons.title: States of Matter
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - The three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Everything around you is matter. Matter is anything that has mass and takes up space. Matter can exist in three states: solid, liquid, and gas. In this lesson, you will learn about the three states of matter and their properties.

## Main Content

**The Three States of Matter**

Solids have a fixed shape and volume. A rock keeps its shape no matter where you put it. Liquids have a fixed volume but take the shape of their container. Water fills a cup or a bucket depending on the container. Gases have no fixed shape or volume. Air fills whatever space is available.

blocks.content: Everything around you is matter. Matter can exist in three states: solid, liquid, and gas. In this lesson, you will learn about the three states of matter and their properties.

terms.definition: Anything that has mass and takes up space
A state of matter with a fixed shape and volume
A state of matter with a fixed volume that takes the shape of its container
A state of matter that fills whatever space is available
The amount of space that matter takes up

lessons.titleThai: สถานะของสสาร

lessons.title: Matter Everywhere
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Identifying matter in everyday objects and understanding that all objects are made of matter.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Matter is everywhere! Your desk, the air you breathe, the water you drink, and even you are all made of matter. In this lesson, you will discover that all objects around you are made of matter in different states.

## Main Content

**Matter in Daily Life**

Look around your classroom. The desk is made of wood, which is a solid. The water in your bottle is a liquid. The air you breathe is a gas. Even your pencil, your shoes, and the whiteboard are all made of matter. Everything that you can see, touch, or feel is matter.

blocks.content: Matter is everywhere! Your desk, the air you breathe, the water you drink, and even you are all made of matter. In this lesson, you will discover that all objects around you are made of matter in different states.

terms.definition: A thing that can be seen or touched
The amount of matter in an object
The form that matter takes: solid, liquid, or gas

lessons.titleThai: สสารอยู่รอบตัวเรา

lessons.title: Materials Lab
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Hands-on investigation comparing properties of different materials.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Today you will investigate different materials and compare their properties. You will observe, touch, and test materials to learn how scientists describe and classify them.

## Main Content

**Lab Activity**

Gather materials such as wood, metal, plastic, cloth, and paper. Observe each material carefully. Describe its color, shape, texture, and size. Test whether it is hard or soft, rough or smooth. Record your observations in a table.

blocks.content: Today you will investigate different materials and compare their properties. You will observe, touch, and test materials to learn how scientists describe and classify them.

terms.definition: To study something carefully to learn more about it

terms.thai: เปรียบเทียบ
terms.definition: To look at how things are the same or different
To write down observations during an experiment

lessons.titleThai: ห้องปฏิบัติการวัสดุ

lessons.title: Changing Materials
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - How materials can be changed by heating, cooling, mixing, and cutting.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Materials can change in many ways. Ice melts when it gets warm. Water boils when it gets hot. Paper can be cut and folded. In this lesson, you will learn about different ways materials can be changed.

## Main Content

**Ways Materials Change**

Heating can melt solids into liquids. Cooling can freeze liquids into solids. Mixing two materials can create something new. Cutting, bending, and folding can change a material's shape. Some changes are temporary, while others are permanent.

blocks.content: Materials can change in many ways. Ice melts when it gets warm. Water boils when it gets hot. Paper can be cut and folded. In this lesson, you will learn about different ways materials can be changed.

terms.definition: To change from a solid to a liquid by heating
To change from a liquid to a solid by cooling
To combine two or more materials together
A change that cannot be undone

lessons.title: Unit 3 Review
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Review activity for properties and states of matter.
lessons.content:
## Review Activity

Test your knowledge of materials and their properties! Sort materials into groups, identify states of matter, and describe properties of everyday objects.

blocks.content: Test your knowledge of materials and their properties! Sort materials into groups, identify states of matter, and describe properties of everyday objects.

terms.definition: To look at something again to remember it
To organize things into groups

lessons.title: Unit 3 Summative Assessment
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Summative assessment covering material properties and states of matter.
lessons.content:
## Summative Assessment

This assessment covers everything you learned about matter and materials in Unit 3. You will be tested on describing materials, classifying them by properties, and understanding the three states of matter.

blocks.content: This assessment covers everything you learned about matter and materials in Unit 3. Good luck!

lessons.titleThai: แบบทดสอบปลายหน่วย หน่วย 3

lessons.title: Matter Sorting Game
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Fun game sorting objects by their properties and states of matter.
lessons.content:
## Review Game

Play a fun sorting game! Sort everyday objects into the correct state of matter: solid, liquid, or gas. Practice describing materials by their properties and see how quickly you can classify them correctly.

blocks.content: Play a fun sorting game! Sort everyday objects into the correct state of matter: solid, liquid, or gas.

terms.definition: An activity you play for fun and learning
To organize things into groups

lessons.titleThai: เกมจัดกลุ่มสสาร

lessons.title: Materials in Our Life
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - How materials are used in everyday life.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Materials are all around us and are used in many ways. The clothes we wear, the food we eat, and the buildings we live in are all made from different materials. In this lesson, you will learn how materials are used in daily life.

## Main Content

**Using Materials Wisely**

We choose materials based on their properties. We make cooking pots from metal because metal conducts heat well. We make windows from glass because glass is transparent. We make raincoats from plastic because plastic keeps water out. Understanding material properties helps us use the right material for the right job.

blocks.content: Materials are all around us and are used in many ways. The clothes we wear, the food we eat, and the buildings we live in are all made from different materials.

terms.definition: A special quality of a material

terms.thai: มีประโยชน์
terms.definition: Something that helps you do something
To allow heat or electricity to pass through

lessons.titleThai: วัสดุในชีวิตประจำวัน

lessons.title: Science Journal: Materials
lessons.description: Grade 3 MATTER & MATERIALS - Recording observations and findings about materials in a science journal.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Scientists keep careful records of their observations and experiments. Today you will use a science journal to record everything you learned about materials in this unit.

## Main Content

**What to Record**

Record your observations about different materials. Draw pictures of materials you investigated. Write down the properties you discovered. Note which materials are solids, liquids, and gases. Your science journal is a valuable tool for organizing your scientific knowledge.

blocks.content: Scientists keep careful records of their observations and experiments. Today you will use a science journal to record everything you learned about materials in this unit.

terms.definition: A book where you record observations and ideas
Information you gather using your senses
To write down information for later use
